etymology.description: The word 爸爸 (bàba, "dad; father") is an example of a reduplicative form in Chinese, where the character 爸 (bà, "dad") is repeated for emphasis or to create a familiar, affectionate tone.
etymology.interpretation: The character 爸 (bà, "dad") is a colloquial term for father, conveying a sense of warmth and closeness. In 爸爸 (bàba, "dad; father"), the repetition of 爸 emphasizes affection and familiarity, typical of reduplicative forms used in many languages to denote intimacy or childish affection.

examples.chinese: 我爸爸很喜欢钓鱼(我爸爸很喜歡釣魚)。
爸爸每天早上跑步(爸爸每天早上跑步)，所以身体很好(所以身體很好)。
examples.pinyin: Bàba měitiān zǎoshang pǎobù, suǒyǐ shēntǐ hěn hǎo.
examples.english: Dad runs every morning, so he is in great health.

examples.chinese: 我会和爸爸一起去旅行(我會和爸爸一起去旅行)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ huì hé bàba yīqǐ qù lǚxíng.
examples.english: I will go traveling with my dad.
